                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05         TVENCENDO-W         PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05          TVAF3-W             PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05          TVAF4-W             PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05          TNOVADO4-W          PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05          TNOVADO-W           PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
           05          TSEMNOV-W           PIC 9(13)V99
                                       OCCURS 14 TIMES.
      *
       05          DATA-AUX            PIC 9(08).
       05          DATA-AUX-R REDEFINES  DATA-AUX.
           10      DIA-AUX             PIC 9(02).
           10      MES-AUX             PIC 9(02).
           10      ANO-AUX             PIC 9(04).
      *
       05          DATA-CAB            PIC 9(08).
       05          RDATA-CAB REDEFINES DATA-CAB.
           10      DIA-CAB             PIC 9(02).
           10      MES-CAB             PIC 9(02).
           10      ANO-CAB             PIC 9(04).

       05 IND-W                        PIC 9(02) VALUE ZEROS.
       05 WS-EXISTE                    PIC 9(01) VALUE ZEROS.
       05 SIT-W                        PIC X(03) VALUE SPACES.
       05 WS-PRIM                      PIC 9(01) VALUE ZEROS.
       05 MASC-W                       PIC ZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
       05 QTD-MASC                     PIC ZZ9 VALUE ZEROS.
       05 WS-LIN-SOBR                  PIC 9(02) VALUE ZEROS.
       05 WS-LIN-MAX                   PIC 9(02) VALUE 66.          
      *
      *****
      *
      *
      ****** R E L A T O R I O *****
      *
       01          LIN-W1.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(113) VALUE
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(61) VALUE
              'FAMILIA PAULISTA COMPANHIA HIPOTECARIA  '.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(12)  VALUE
                   'DATA  -'.
           05      DATA-W1             PIC 99/9999.
      *
       01          LIN-W2.
           05      CAB-W2              PIC X(61) VALUE
      *    'RELACAO DOS CONTRATOS ALTERADOS NO MES DE '.
           'QUADRO GERENCIAL'.
      *    05      MES-W2              PIC X(09).
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE '/'.
      *    05      ANO-W2              PIC 9(04)B(10).
      *
       01          LIN-W3.
           05      FILLER             PIC X(113) VALUE
      *    05      FILLER             PIC X(61) VALUE
                   'D.T.I. - SFH017'.
           05      FILLER             PIC X(16)  VALUE
                   'FOLHA -'.
           05      FOLHA-W3           PIC 9(03)  VALUE ZEROS.
      *
       01          LIN-W4.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(132) VALUE ALL '-'.
      *
       01          LIN-W5.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(14) VALUE
                   'SITUACAO'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(09) VALUE
                   'QTDE'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(07) VALUE
                   'TR'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(14) VALUE
                   'SLD. VENCIDO'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(24) VALUE
                   'SLD. VINCENDO'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(15) VALUE
                   'VAF3'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(08) VALUE
                   'VAF4'.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(14) VALUE
                   'SLD. NOVADO'.
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(22) VALUE
      *            'SALDO NOVADO'.
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(12) VALUE
      *            'SALDO'.
      *
      *01          LIN-W5A.
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(111) VALUE SPACES.
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(10) VALUE
      *            '- VAF4'.
      *    05      FILLER              PIC X(11) VALUE
      *            'SEM NOVACAO'.
      * 
       01          DET-W5B.       
           05      SIT-W5B             PIC X(20).
           05      DIV1-W5B            PIC X(01) VALUE "(".
           05      TR-W5B              PIC X(05).
           05      DIV2-W5B            PIC X(01) VALUE ")".
      *
       01          DET-W5.
           05      SIT-W5              PIC X(15).
           05      QTD-W5              PIC X(12).
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
           05      VENCIDO-W5          PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
           05      VENCENDO-W5         PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
           05      VAF3-W5             PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
           05      VAF4-W5             PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
           05      TNOVADO4-W5         PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
      *    05      TNOVADO-W5          PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
     **    05      FILLER              PIC X(01) VALUE SPACES.
     **    05      SLDSEMNOV-W5        PIC ZZZ.ZZZ.ZZ9,99.
       01 SITUACAO-T.
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "RCV".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "TOTAL RCV".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "RNV".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "TOTAL RNV".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "TOTAL".                 
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "RCV + RNV".                                  
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "SM".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "RNV (NEGATIVA)".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "TOTAL SM + RNV".                                  
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "CNH".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "NEGATIVA".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "COBERTURA".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "AUDITADO".
                 05 FILLER PIC X(15) VALUE "NAO AUDITADO".
        01 SITUACAO-R REDEFINES SITUACAO-T.
                 05 SITUACAO-W              PIC X(15) OCCURS 14 TIMES.

       
                     
      *
       COPY TABMES.
      *
       01  LINHA-BRANCO.
           05      FILLER              PIC X(136) VALUE ALL ' '.
      /
        SCREEN SECTION.
        01 TELA-W.
                03 BLANK SCREEN.
                03 LINE 1 COL 20 VALUE
      -  "FAMILIA PAULISTA COMPANHIA HIPOTECARIA  " HIGHLIGHT.
                03 LINE 3 COL 20 VALUE
      -  "SISTEMA     - S F H" HIGHLIGHT.
                03 LINE 5 COL 20 VALUE
      -  "SUB-SISTEMA - QUADRO GERENCIAL SFHCEF" HIGHLIGHT.
                03 LINE 7 COL 20 VALUE
      -  "PROGRAMA    - SFH017" HIGHLIGHT.
                03 LINE 12 COL 20 VALUE
      -  "I M P R I M I N D O   ! ! !" HIGHLIGHT.

        01 LABO-LIMPA.
                03 LINE 25 COL 1 BLANK LINE.
        01  LIMPA-TELA.
                03  BLANK SCREEN.
        PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ********************
      *
       001-INICIO SECTION.
       001-00.
           PERFORM 100-PROCINIC.
           PERFORM 200-PROCESSA UNTIL BOSFH06KEY-S = HIGH-VALUES.
           PERFORM 600-TOTAL
                   VARYING IND-W FROM 1 BY 1
                   UNTIL IND-W GREATER THAN 10.
           PERFORM 999-FIM.
           STOP    RUN.
       001-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *****
      *
       100-PROCINIC SECTION.
       100-00.
           DISPLAY TELA-W.

           SORT SORT-FILE
                   ON ASCENDING KEY S-NOME
                   USING SFHCEF
                   GIVING SFHCEF-S.

           OPEN    INPUT  SFHCEF-S.
           OPEN    INPUT  SFHCAD.
           OPEN    OUTPUT RELATO.
       100-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *****
      *
       200-PROCESSA SECTION.
       200-00.

           PERFORM 800-LE-SFHCEF.
           IF      BOSFH06KEY-S = HIGH-VALUES
                   GO TO 200-EXIT.

           MOVE BOSFH06EMP-S     TO BOSFH01EMP.
           MOVE BOSFH06NRO-S     TO BOSFH01NRO.
           MOVE BOSFH06DV-S      TO BOSFH01DV.

           PERFORM 700-PROCURA-SFHCAD.

           IF WS-EXISTE EQUAL 1
                   GO TO 200-EXIT.

           PERFORM 300-INICIA.

           PERFORM 400-VERIFICA-CONDICOES.

       200-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *****
      *
       300-INICIA SECTION.
       300-00.

      *    MOVE MESTAB-W(BOSFH06MBASE-S) TO MES-W2.
      *    MOVE BOSFH06ABASE-S TO ANO-W2.

           MOVE  01            TO DIA-CAB
           MOVE BOSFH06MBASE-S TO MES-CAB
           MOVE BOSFH06ABASE-S TO ANO-CAB.

           MOVE    DATA-CAB  TO DATA-W1.

           IF WS-PRIM EQUAL ZEROS
               PERFORM 500-CABEC.
       300-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       400-VERIFICA-CONDICOES SECTION.
       400-00.
           MOVE BOSFH01POSICAO TO SIT-W.
      *    MOVE BOSFH01CONDRET TO TR-W5.

           EVALUATE SIT-W
           WHEN "RCV"
               IF BOSFH06AUDINAUD-S EQUAL "AUDI"
                     MOVE 1 TO IND-W
                     PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
               ELSE
                     MOVE 2 TO IND-W
                     PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
               END-IF
               MOVE 11 TO IND-W
               PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES               
               MOVE 13 TO IND-W
               PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES                              
      * RNV PODE SER AUDITADO/NAO AUDITADO E NEGATIVADO
           WHEN "RNV"
               IF BOSFH06AUDINAUD-S EQUAL "AUDI"
                   IF BOSFH01CONDRET EQUAL 05
                          MOVE 7 TO IND-W
                          PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                          MOVE 14 TO IND-W
                          PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                   ELSE
                          MOVE 3 TO IND-W
                          PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                          MOVE 12 TO IND-W
                          PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                          MOVE 13 TO IND-W
                          PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                   END-IF
               ELSE
                  IF BOSFH06AUDINAUD-S EQUAL "NAUD"
                       IF BOSFH01CONDRET EQUAL 05
                           MOVE 8 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                           MOVE 14 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                       ELSE
                           MOVE 4 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                           MOVE 12 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                           MOVE 13 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                       END-IF

                  ELSE
                       IF BOSFH01CONDRET EQUAL 05
                           MOVE 10 TO IND-W
                           PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                       END-IF
                  END-IF
               END-IF
           WHEN "SM "
           WHEN " SM"
      *
      * SM NEGATIVADO (AUDI OU NAUDI) ADICIONA SOMENTE NOS NEGATIVADOS
      *
               IF BOSFH01CONDRET EQUAL 05
                   MOVE 10 TO IND-W
                   PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
               ELSE
                   IF BOSFH06AUDINAUD-S EQUAL "AUDI"
                       MOVE 5 TO IND-W
                       PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                   ELSE
                       MOVE 6 TO IND-W
                       PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
                   END-IF
                   MOVE 14 TO IND-W
                   PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
               END-IF
           WHEN "CNH"
               MOVE 9 TO IND-W
               PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
      * NENHUMA DAS SITUACOES ANTERIORES
           WHEN OTHER
               IF BOSFH01CONDRET EQUAL 05
                       MOVE 10 TO IND-W
                       PERFORM 450-ADICIONA-VALORES
               ELSE
                       DISPLAY BOSFH01POSICAO AT 2610
                       DISPLAY 'SITUACAO INVALIDA' AT 2510
                       ACCEPT ACC-W
               END-IF
           END-EVALUATE.
       400-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       450-ADICIONA-VALORES SECTION.
       450-00.
           ADD BOSFH06SLDVENCIDO-S  TO TVENCIDO-W(IND-W).
           ADD BOSFH06SLDVENCENDO-S TO TVENCENDO-W(IND-W).
           ADD BOSFH06VAF3POSIC-S   TO TVAF3-W(IND-W).
           ADD BOSFH06VAF4POSIC-S   TO TVAF4-W(IND-W).
           ADD 1                  TO QTD-W(IND-W).

           COMPUTE SALDO-W = BOSFH01VCEFMANT + BOSFH01CRE -
                   BOSFH01DEB + BOSFH01CMONETCEF
                   + BOSFH01JUROSCEF + BOSFH01VAGMANT
                   + BOSFH01CMONETAG + BOSFH01JUROSAG.

                   ADD SALDO-W TO TSEMNOV-W(IND-W).

       450-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       500-CABEC SECTION.
       500-00.
             MOVE ZEROS TO LINAGE-COUNTER.
             ADD     1 TO FOLHA-W3.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W1.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W2.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W3.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LINHA-BRANCO.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W4.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W5.
      *      WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W5A.
             WRITE   LINHA FROM LIN-W4.
             MOVE 1 TO WS-PRIM.
       500-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       600-TOTAL SECTION.
       600-00.
      *
      * LINHA DE SITUACAO
      *

       EVALUATE IND-W
       WHEN 01
            MOVE "RCV" TO SIT-W5B
                        MOVE "12-17" TO TR-W5B
            WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
*******************************************************                        
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                        
            MOVE "AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES            
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA
       WHEN 02
            MOVE "NAO AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES                        
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************            
            MOVE "TOTAL RCV" TO SIT-W5
            MOVE 11 TO IND-W
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************                        
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4            
*******************************************************                        
            MOVE 02 TO IND-W
       WHEN 03       
            MOVE "RNV" TO SIT-W5B
                        MOVE "12-17" TO TR-W5B
            WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
*****************************************************                        
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*****************************************************            
            MOVE "AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA
       WHEN 04
            MOVE "NAO AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES            
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************            
            MOVE "TOTAL RNV" TO SIT-W5
            MOVE 12 TO IND-W
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
            PERFORM 650-GRAVA            
*******************************************************            
            WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************
                        MOVE SPACES TO DIV1-W5B
                                       DIV2-W5B
            MOVE "TOTAL" TO SIT-W5B
                        MOVE SPACES TO TR-W5B
            WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
                        MOVE "RCV + RNV" TO SIT-W5
            MOVE 13 TO IND-W
            PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
                        PERFORM 650-GRAVA
                        WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
                        MOVE 04 TO IND-W
                        MOVE "(" TO DIV1-W5B
                        MOVE ")" TO DIV2-W5B

      *
      * A PARTIR DE 20/9/16 LUZIA SOLICITOU ALTERACAO
      *

      *    WHEN 05
      *                 MOVE "SM" TO SIT-W5B
      *                 MOVE "12-17" TO TR-W5B
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
*******************************************************                        
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                        
      *                 MOVE "AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
      *    WHEN 06
      *                 MOVE "NAO AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************                        
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                                    
      *    WHEN 07
      *                 MOVE "RNV (NEGATIVA)" TO SIT-W5B
      *                 MOVE "  17" TO TR-W5B
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
*******************************************************                        
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                                    
      *                 MOVE "AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
      *    WHEN 08
      *                 MOVE "NAO AUDITADO" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************    
      *                 MOVE "TOTAL SM + RNV" TO SIT-W5
      *                 MOVE 14 TO IND-W
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                
      *                 MOVE 08 TO IND-W
      * CNH
      *    WHEN 09
*******************************************************            
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************           
      *                 MOVE "CNH" TO SIT-W5B
      *                 MOVE "  11" TO TR-W5B
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
      *                 MOVE "NAO HOMOLOGADO" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************
      * NEGATIVA DE COBERTURA
      *    WHEN 10
*******************************************************
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************           
      *                 MOVE "NEGATIVA" TO SIT-W5B
      *                 MOVE "  15" TO TR-W5B
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5B
      *                 MOVE "COBERTURA" TO SIT-W5
      *                 PERFORM 620-MOVE-VALORES
      *                 PERFORM 650-GRAVA
*******************************************************            
      *                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
*******************************************************                   
       END-EVALUATE.
       

       600-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       620-MOVE-VALORES SECTION.
       620-00.
           MOVE QTD-W(IND-W)       TO QTD-MASC.
           MOVE QTD-MASC           TO QTD-W5.
           MOVE TVENCIDO-W(IND-W)  TO VENCIDO-W5.
           MOVE TVENCENDO-W(IND-W) TO VENCENDO-W5.
           MOVE TVAF3-W(IND-W)     TO VAF3-W5.
           MOVE TVAF4-W(IND-W)     TO VAF4-W5.

       COMPUTE TNOVADO4-W(IND-W)= TVENCIDO-W(IND-W) + TVENCENDO-W(IND-W)
                                   + TVAF3-W(IND-W) + TVAF4-W(IND-W).

       COMPUTE TNOVADO-W(IND-W) = TVENCIDO-W(IND-W) + TVENCENDO-W(IND-W)
                                             + TVAF3-W(IND-W).

           MOVE TNOVADO4-W(IND-W) TO TNOVADO4-W5.
      *    MOVE TNOVADO-W(IND-W)  TO TNOVADO-W5.
      *    MOVE TSEMNOV-W(IND-W)  TO SLDSEMNOV-W5.
       620-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       650-GRAVA SECTION.
       650-00.
           WRITE LINHA FROM DET-W5
                 AT EOP
                 WRITE LINHA FROM LIN-W4
                 PERFORM 500-CABEC.
       650-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       700-PROCURA-SFHCAD SECTION.
       700-00.

           READ  SFHCAD
                 INVALID KEY
                 MOVE 1 TO WS-EXISTE
                 GO TO 700-EXIT.

      * CESSAO BRADESCO - ESTOU USANDO A MESMA VARIAVEL SOMENTE PARA QUE
      * ELE SAIA QUANDO FOR UM CLIENTE CEDIDO


           IF BOSFH01CESSAO EQUAL 2
              MOVE 1 TO WS-EXISTE
           ELSE
               MOVE 0 TO WS-EXISTE.

       700-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       800-LE-SFHCEF SECTION.
       800-00.

           READ    SFHCEF-S
                   AT END
                   MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO BOSFH06KEY-S.
       800-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
       999-FIM SECTION.
       999-00.
           DISPLAY    'FIM DE PROCESSAMENTO - SFH017' AT LINE 25 COL 20.
           DISPLAY LIMPA-TELA.
           DISPLAY LIMPA-TELA  AT LINE 10 COL 10.
           CLOSE   RELATO.
           CLOSE   SFHCEF-S.
           CLOSE   SFHCAD.
       999-EXIT.
           EXIT.
`

issue_title: Add more node attributes
issue_body: Right now we have basic things like: node.running/startup_config, but it would be nice to automatically create some additional attributes, similar to those found in 'show version'.

issue_title: Active-HDL fails compiling Vunit library (aliases)
issue_body: Problem related to aliases in log_api.vhd

issue_title: A node whose parent is another node is not returned in allLowerNodes
issue_body:
When calling nodes.allLowerNodes the nodes grouped under another node are not returned.  The insteon devices that contain multiple nodes can be grouped together by right clicking one and selecting 'Group Devices'

It looks like nodes need to be changed to contain children, but need to look into it deeper.

issue_title: Undocumented `repository` option on `curator.create_repository()`
issue_body:
In `curator.create_repository()` the code expects a repository kwarg:

https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/master/curator/utils.py#L893
```
    if not 'repository' in kwargs:
        raise MissingArgument('Missing required parameter --repository')
    else:
        repository = kwargs['repository']
```

However, the repository command isn't mentioned in the documentation at all. As best I can tell, the documented parameter `location` is unused -- perhaps this parameter was supposed to be called repository?

issue_title: Serializer doesn't support uuid type
Allow for attaching/detaching multiple disks with a single call
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When a volume uses multiple disks then we'd want to detach and attach the entire set in the same call instead of running requests for each disk in a loop. A single detach/attach can take 3-4 minutes so larger volumes with several disks, say 6-8 disk per volume, result in a 40-60 minute wait time for sequential detach or attach operations.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Azure CLI should support a function that takes a list of disks as a parameter for detach and attach operations.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The Azure CLI calls attach_managed_data_disk which in turns runs get_vm, vm.storage_profile.data_disks.append then set_vm. The alternative would be to loop for an array parameter and append each element before calling set_vm.

**Additional context**
No additional context.

issue_title: [Test] --playback-only option
issue_body:
The current behavior of `run_tests --module` is to playback all tests that have recordings and record all tests that do not (and are not live only).  When I have to re-record networking tests, often there are a few tests that are very slow and block.  During that downtime, I would love to replay the tests that have already been re-recorded and identify which, if any, fail to playback.  If I do this by running the module, I'll inevitably have two processes trying to record the same test, and nothing good comes of that. 

Consider adding a `--playback-only` flag to `run_tests` that will only playback tests, and skips any tests that are live_only (obviously) or aren't but don't have a recording file.

...and the summary of which tests fail at the end of the run would be super super super helpful.

issue_title: Deprecate Application(debug=True) in favor of DEBUG global flag

Currently debug mode is cumbersome:
1. User can enable it by `Application(debug=True)`

Better to deprecate it and always rely on `PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG` environment variable or shiny new `python -X dev` mode (https://docs.python.org/3.7/using/cmdline.html#id5)

issue_title: Remove non-ascii characters from PEM file.
issue_body:
Apparently some JVMs are very strict about non-ascii characters, even in comments. See kennethreitz/requests#3776 for more.

This can be fixed by piping the download through a small Python script:

```python
import sys

for l in sys.stdin.readlines():
    print unicode(l, 'utf8').strip().encode('ascii', errors='backslashreplace')
```

issue_title: Add ability to omit background color option
issue_body:
Now we can't specify font as dark (or bold, underline, blink, reverse, concealed) without changing background color. Meanwhile we can't specify background color to black (due to termcolor library limitations).

```python
DEFAULT_COLORS = {
    'command': 'red on_grey dark',
    'parameter': 'green dark',
}

In [11]: colors_of('command')
Out[11]: ('red', 'on_grey', ['dark'])  # Correct!

In [12]: colors_of('parameter')
Out[12]: ('green', 'dark', [])  # Not correct!
```

So I think we should to improve `colors_of` method.

issue_title: preact, preact-compat, proptypes as bower dependencies vs. npm
issue_body:
Follow up from https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2056: Users are seeing `error: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/PATH/TO/NOTEBOOK/node_modules/proptypes'` in 5.0.0rc1.

The notebook's only npm dependencies after being [unwebpackified](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/pull/2011) is preact, preact-compat, and proptypes. Is there any reason why we can't get these dependencies from bower? There probably is but I just want to make sure. 

1. Install preact, etc. from npm: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/271c4b1f279901e06f522a22a2521eb9a9e6c20b/package.json#L22-L23
2. Copy npm packages from `node_modules` to `components` directory: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/setupbase.py#L383
3. Import preact, etc. using script tags: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/326077fa2d7addd646de2302217f8cff4e9c8d5d/notebook/templates/page.html#L19-L21
4. Use preact, etc. in ShortcutEditor: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/c305d8fda3b5f8bfb8c1ce520c0a6927f56261a1/notebook/static/notebook/js/shortcuteditor.js#L36-L169

cc @minrk @blink1073

issue_title: Where should we be storing frontend config?
issue_body:
- I thought we had agreed frontend config would go in platform specific directories (because users won't normally edit it directly), e.g. `~/.config/jupyter/nbconfig/` on Linux.
- Migration puts it in `~/.jupyter/nbconfig/`
- The notebook server looks for the files just in `~/.jupyter/`

We definitely need to ensure that the migration tool and the notebook server use the same folder for it. I think it's the notebook server that's wrong, because those files should have their own folder namespace.

## Background

* I'm on OSX
* I have Indy running locally, following the instructions from https://github.com/hyperledger/indy-node/blob/master/environment/docker/pool/README.md with default parameters
* I compiled *libindy* as described in https://github.com/hyperledger/indy-sdk/blob/master/doc/mac-build.md and the build completed successfully
* I pointed at my local build/check-out of indy-sdk using LD_LIBRARY_PATH and PYTHONPATH

I tried many different ways of connecting to Indy using the Python wrapper but it always fails with _CommonInvalidState_.

I found this issue report: https://jira.hyperledger.org/browse/IS-355?page=com.atlassian.streams.streams-jira-plugin%3Aactivity-stream-issue-tab

My suspicion is that the version of indy-crypto may be inconsistent between indy-sdk and indy-node, but I can't see the way to roll it back for indy-sdk without losing the whole lot of recent changes.

Any guidance how to fix it would be highly appreciated.

Stan

issue_title: Planemo and the last conda version?
issue_body:
Hi,

I have an issue with the version of conda needed by Galaxy 3.19.3. (in a first place with my travis test and in a second place with Galaxy itself).
One of my dependency `bioconductor-camera` take ages to `Solving package specifications: ...............................................` to finally reach the TravisCI time out. I think it's a problem with conflict between R versions.

- To investiguate, I choose a really easy package `r-batch`
```bash
./miniconda2-conda3.19.3/bin/conda create --name __r-batch@1.1_4 r-batch=1.1_4 --override-channels --channel bioconda --channel r --channel defaults --channel conda-forge
```
Installation the `r3.2.2` version
`r-batch:       1.1_4-r3.2.2_0`

- I  have to remove the conda-forge channel
```bash
./miniconda2-conda3.19.3/bin/conda create --name __r-batch@1.1_4 r-batch=1.1_4 --override-channels --channel bioconda --channel r --channel defaults
```
Installation
`r-batch:       1.1_4-r3.3.1_0`

- No  problemo with the last 4.2.12
```bash
./miniconda2-conda4.2.12/bin/conda create --name __r-batch@1.1_4 r-batch=1.1_4 --override-channels --channel bioconda --channel r --channel defaults --channel conda-forge
```
Installation
`r-batch:       1.1_4-r3.3.1_0   bioconda`

I know that there is an issue with the `offline` option needed by Galaxy or something like that.



My question is: is it possible to use the last version of conda now with planemo, or is it still mandatory? According to https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/3227 ?

My last possibility is to alter this file : `.planemo/condarc` and maybe the galaxy.ini `conda_ensure_channels`of my docker image

issue_title: Port parallel test runner from Toil
Increase executor shutdown grace period
issue_body: When the framework asks the executor to shutdown, the slave waits 5s (default) for the executor to shut down before forcefully killing it. Increasing the value allows for more time to conduct cleanup jobs once the executor is asked to shutdown.

issue_title: Don't run chunking logic with just one chunk
issue_body: This is very related to #104 (which I think is mostly resolved).  If we only end up with single fq chunk, it still goes through pigz (copy of fastq) and the gam chunking/merging functions.  These aren't super expensive timewise but can eat a lot of disk on big inputs.

issue_title: Fix network agent assignment

issue_title: API break in rrule.weekday 2.6.0
issue_body:
This commit bced9222a9caae04924e43b9ffb2d2cdb2488174 changes the signature of rrule.weekday constructor from `(weekday, n=None)` to `(wkday, n=None)`, which breaks compatibility for code explicitly calling the constructor like this: `rrule.weekday(weekday=2)`.

Looking at the code it does not look trivial to fix though, thanks to the class being called like the argument :/

issue_title: parser.parse doesn't handle tzinfo passed in as tzinfos object
issue_body:
This code here in parser.parse appears to be wrong:

        if not ignoretz:
            if (isinstance(tzinfos, collections.Callable) or
                    tzinfos and res.tzname in tzinfos):

If tzinfos is just a datetime.tzinfo object, this will raise an error like this:
`TypeError: argument of type 'Europe/London' is not iterable`
This is because the 'in' operator doesn't work on tzinfo objects. But the type check here doesn't prevent that.

issue_title: Should cloudpickle.dumps fail whenclosing over something undefined?
issue_body:
```python
def f():
    undefined_function()  # this is not defined

import cloudpickle
cloudpickle.dumps(f)  # I think this should really throw an exception.
```

The last line succeeds, but I wonder if it would be preferable to raise an exception instead (instead of waiting for it to fail in the corresponding call to `cloudpickle.loads`). Alternatively, is there a way for the caller to configure cloudpickle to raise an exception in the above situation?

issue_title: Gromacs 5.x MDrunner class implementation incompatibilities
issue_body:
I have found two errors in regards to the MDrunner class and the `check_mdrun_success` function found in `run.py`.

1. Command passed to subprocess in the class method `run` does not include `gmx`, resulting in an `OSError`
2. `check_mdrun_success` searches the last couple of lines of the log file for the string `"Finished mdrun on node"` at the start of the line, while a successful run writes `"Finished mdrun on rank"`

My temporary fixes to these were as follows:
1. 
```python
cmd = self.commandline(**mpiargs) 
cmd = ['gmx'] + cmd
```
But this is clearly a sloppy solution while breaking Gromacs 4.x usage
2. Simply change `if line.startswith("Finished mdrun on node")` to `if line.startswith("Finished mdrun on rank")`

issue_title: nteract as a web app
issue_body:
This is a bit of a high level overview of what we need to make nteract available as a web application in addition to components and modules for people to build their own (see #301) or for using our transforms in a standard way in JupyterLab (as @gnestor has done by grabbing the code and maintaining separately).

We ended up bringing in `react-jupyter-display-area` as well as `react-transformime` into this repository which helped us iterate faster and create a tighter build. We swore that we would export these again as part of a monorepo and that time is certainly upon us.

Here's how I imagine this repository will become laid out, as a top level:

```
packages/ # lerna style modules
electronApp/ # uses the lerna modules to compose the electron app
webApp/ # uses the lerna modules to compose a web app
```

My assumption is that the web app will build upon the jupyter notebook server. I'm (personally) not trying to support a standalone single user server for nteract, the primary reason I think I have to build a standalone frontend is to make sure that the exported components are useful while working on [a multi user server](https://github.com/nteract/commuter).

## Precursors

In order to ease maintenance burden of these modules, we've been decoupling components and reducers while also stabilizing our base. In progress issues:

* [ ] [Refactor components to remove Redux logic](https://github.com/nteract/nteract/issues/1288)

## Web app necessities

* [ ] Menu components
  * [ ] Create mocks in https://github.com/nteract/mocks
  * [ ] Develop components with callbacks (that someone can hook up to dispatch events for the notebook)
* [ ] Dashboard - in progress in [commuter](https://github.com/nteract/commuter)

--------------------------------------

I'll continue to update the above with an outline as we push towards this. What else is a necessity here?

issue_title: All the optional datasets need to be given
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

Sample compositor config
```yaml
  my_composite:
    compositor: !!python/name:my_composites.MyCompositor
    prerequisites:
      - 10.8
    optional_prerequisites:
      - solar_zenith_angle
      - 0.6
```

The compositor for above config
```python
class MyCompositor(CompositeBase):

    def __call__(self, projectables=None, optional_datasets=None, **info):
        data_1 = projectables[0].copy()
        sza, data_2 = optional_datasets
        # If data 2 not available, use plain IR image
        if data_2 is None:
            return Dataset(data_1, **info.copy())
        # If SZA not available, calculate them
        if sza is None:
            from pyorbital.astronomy import sun_zenith_angle
            sza = sun_zenith_angle(data_1.info["start_time"], *data_1.info["area"].get_lonlats())
        # Use data 2 where Sun is high enough
        data_1[sza < 90] = data_2[sza < 90]
        return Dataset(data_1, **info.copy())
```

Test script
```python
from satpy import Scene
import glob
# Use data that does no yield solar_zenith_angle, and/or remove 0.6 um data
fnames = glob.glob("/path/to/data/*")
glbl = Scene(filenames=fnames)
glbl.load(['my_composite'])
```

#### Problem description

If, when creating the above composite, either of the optional datasets are unavailable, the `optional_dataset` is a list of length one, and thus the unpacking fails. Also, the missing dataset can't be determined as the order is critical.

#### Expected Output

1. With all data available: composite with night-side data from dataset 1 and day-side from dataset 2
2. With SZA unavailable: composite with night-side data from dataset 1 and day-side from dataset 2
3. With dataset 1 missing: composite with everything from dataset 1
4. With SZA and dataset 2 missing: composite with everything from dataset 1

#### Actual Result, Traceback if applicable

For the above list:
1. works as planned
2. `ValueError: need more than 1 values to unpack`
3. `ValueError: need more than 1 values to unpack`
4. `ValueError: need more than 0 values to unpack`

#### Versions of Python, package at hand and relevant dependencies
Current SatPy develop branch

issue_title: graphene swallows exceptions during data fetching, destroying info about them in the process.
issue_body:
Currently learning graphene the hard way, and I've been trying to get some info out of it via `print` and, when that didn't work, by raising exceptions.  It appears that custom exceptions make the query partly fail (it seems to return `None` in place of the object that fails and continues going), but do not get bubbled up at all.

For example:

```
class Query(ObjectType): # everything should be a relay.Node but this, since it's the base of our schema.
    user = Field(User) # current user
    node = relay.NodeField()

    @resolve_only_args
    def resolve_user(self, **args):
        raise Exception(args)
        return User(id=1, user_id=1) # todo

schema = Schema(query=Query, auto_camelcase=False)
```

with the following test code:

```
In [8]: res2 = schema.execute("query { user {user_id} }")

In [9]: print res2.errors, res2.invalid, res2.data
[GraphQLError('{}',)] False OrderedDict([('user', None)])
```

shows that graphql is clearly swallowing custom exceptions, and then returning them as part of `errors`; doing this appears to lose the stack trace (and also makes `sys.exc_info()` useless).  This makes it impossible to integrate with my usual exception monitoring, since we need either the original `exc_info` or the ability to report or reraise the original exception when it's first caught.  While the query being executed is useful to know about, having the original stack trace is far more valuable and only the "which piece of the query was being executed" is really unique - our exception handling code already handles getting request params for us.

Any of the following solutions would work for me:
1) don't swallow the exceptions, just raise them; this avoids doing extra work, since we might want to just 500 these failing request anyway to encourage developers to notice & fix them.  Maybe could be implemented with a Plugin?
2) return the `exc_info` of each exception as part of `errors`.  This seems simplest.
3) have some sort of `on_exception` hook availabe that would run within the `except` block that's currently swallowing these exceptions, that could handle the reporting.

issue_title: SQLAlchemyObjectType{Meta} should be more friendly to subclassing implementations.
issue_body:
Currently, `SQLAlchemyObjectTypeMeta` has the following bits that complicate attempts at subclassing it to  extend functionality:

1.  [This code](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-sqlalchemy/blob/2de70b422eb2141f864ae0388aa79b74f2786d08/graphene_sqlalchemy/types.py#L72) is the only way to suppress all of the 'metaclass magic':

```python
        if not is_base_type(bases, SQLAlchemyObjectTypeMeta):
            return type.__new__(cls, name, bases, attrs)
```

2. It's generally a mess if you want to add your own options to the `Meta` class.  A subclass can do so by deleting them from `attrs['Meta']` beforehand and doing `setattr(cls._meta, option, value)` after calling the superclass, but that's a bit clunky.



The first limitation becomes an issue when doing things like this:

`class MyReplacementSQLAlchemyObjectType(SQLAlchemyObjectType, metaclass=MyReplacementMetaclass):`

when the class isn't supposed to map to a model but rather it's intending to add functionality for its own subclasses.

A few possible solutions to this could be:

- Suppressing the metaclass magic (i.e. returning type.__new__) if the new class subclasses `AbstractType`

- Suppressing the metaclass magic if the class defines `__abstract__ = True` as an attribute (and deleting the attribute).  SQLAlchemy does something like this.



The second issue is something I've seen addressed by having a selectable `Options` subclass or factory rather than the hardcoded Options() that exists now.  (Marshmallow uses something like this).  in   This would require some architectural changes in graphene as well though.  One implementation of a revamped options might simply be something like:

```python
class Options(object):
    setting = "some default value"
    setting2 = "another default value"
  
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        invalid = []
        for k, v in kwargs:
            if not hasattr(self, k):
                invalid.append(k)
            else:
                setattr(self, k, v)
    
        if invalid:        
            raise TypeError(
                "Invalid attributes: {}".format(', '.join(sorted(kwargs.keys()))))

class SQLAlchemyOptions(Options):
   model = None
   # ...
   # No __init__ required, though it could be used to perform validation rather than having it at the metaclass level.
```

Then: 

- Options subclasses can simply add class-level variables to add new options (or change defaults of old ones) to those supported.

- Metaclasses can use attrs['OPTIONS_CLASS'] as the class to use instead of Options.  If it's undefined (which it will be 99% of the time), they use the first entry in `bases` to define it instead.

Marshmallow uses an approach similar to this.

issue_title: Frozen Network w/Universal Sentence Encoder throws `table not initialized` error in Tensorflow serving call
issue_body:
### Problem
Tensorflow serving throws and error that tables are not initialized when freezing a trained graph and quantizing weights despite adding `init_all_tables` arg to freeze graph method.

### Setup
`example.py`
```python

import os
import numpy as np
import tensorflow as tf
import tensorflow_hub as hub

from configs import config
from sklearn.utils import check_X_y


class Example:
    def __init__(self,
                 model_name="Example",
                 embeddings="https://tfhub.dev/google/universal-sentence-encoder/1",
                 model_dir=".",
                 num_epochs=10,
                 batch_size=32,
                 trainable_embeddings=False,
                 learning_rate=0.001,
                 hidden_units=[256, 128, 64],
                 max_steps=10000,
                 embedding_column="text",
                 trainable_embedding=False,
                 tf_random_seed=143,
                 log_step_count_steps=100,
                 save_summary_steps=100,
                 save_checkpoints_steps=100,
                 **kwargs):

        self.hparams = tf.contrib.training.HParams(
            num_epochs=num_epochs,
            batch_size=batch_size,
            trainable_embeddings=trainable_embeddings,
            learning_rate=learning_rate,
            hidden_units=hidden_units,
            max_steps=max_steps,
            embedding_column=embedding_column,
            trainable_embedding=trainable_embedding)

        self.run_config = tf.estimator.RunConfig(
            model_dir=os.path.join(model_dir, model_name),
            tf_random_seed=tf_random_seed,
            log_step_count_steps=log_step_count_steps,
            save_summary_steps=save_summary_steps,
            save_checkpoints_steps=save_checkpoints_steps,
            **kwargs)

        self.embeddings = embeddings
        self.feature_columns = self._create_feature_column()

    def train(self, X, y, labels):
        self.labels = labels
        self.hparams.num_classes = len(self.labels)
        _X, _y = check_X_y(X, y, ensure_2d=False)
        features = {self.hparams.embedding_column: X}
        self.estimator = self._create_estimator()

        self.estimator.train(input_fn=lambda: self._input_fn(
            features=features,
            labels=y,
            mode=tf.estimator.ModeKeys.TRAIN))

    def _create_estimator(self):
        feature_columns = self._create_feature_column()
        optimizer = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(learning_rate=self.hparams.learning_rate)
        return tf.estimator.DNNClassifier(
            feature_columns=feature_columns,
            n_classes=self.hparams.num_classes,
            hidden_units=self.hparams.hidden_units,
            label_vocabulary=self.labels,
            optimizer=optimizer,
            config=self.run_config)

    def _create_feature_column(self):
        text_embeding_column = hub.text_embedding_column(
            self.hparams.embedding_column,
            self.embeddings,
            trainable=self.hparams.trainable_embedding)

        feature_columns = [text_embeding_column]
        return feature_columns

    def _input_fn(self, features, labels, mode=tf.estimator.ModeKeys.TRAIN):
        buffer_size = 2 * self.hparams.batch_size + 1
        shuffle = True if mode == tf.estimator.ModeKeys.TRAIN else False
        dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((features, labels))
        if shuffle:
            dataset = dataset.shuffle(buffer_size)
            dataset = dataset.apply(
                tf.contrib.data.batch_and_drop_remainder(
                    self.hparams.batch_size))
            dataset = dataset.repeat(self.hparams.num_epochs)
            dataset = dataset.prefetch(buffer_size)
            return dataset

    def serving_input_receiver_fn(self):
        receiver_tensor = {
            self.hparams.embedding_column: tf.placeholder(
                dtype=tf.string, shape=[None])}
        return tf.estimator.export.ServingInputReceiver(
            receiver_tensor, receiver_tensor)

    def export_saved_model(self, name="model"):
        self.estimator.export_savedmodel(
            name,
            serving_input_receiver_fn=self.serving_input_receiver_fn)

    def get_data(self):
        X = np.array([
            ["what is the weather like today"],
            ["call mom please"],
            ["do I have any events on my calendar today"],
        ])
        y = np.array(["weather", "contacts", "events"])
        labels = list(set(y.tolist()))
        return X, y, labels


def main(argv=None):
     tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.INFO)
     ex = Example()
     x, y, l = ex.get_data()
     ex.train(x, y, l)
     ex.export_saved_model()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

        transforms=["freeze_graph", "quantize_weights"],

issue_title: max grpc message size is too small
issue_body:
I am using TensorBoard 1.8.0.  I am trying to use the debugger, but I get this error message:

```
grpc._channel._Rendezvous: <_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, Received message larger than max (6561349 vs. 4194304))>
```

Someone asked about this problem here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49648395/tensorboard-debugger-and-grpc-max-message-size-between-client-and-server

A comment on that question suggests that setting `grpc.max_receive_message_size` could help, but there is no variable named `max_receive_message_size` defined by the grpc module, and defining one doesn't do anything.  So I guess this limit needs to be set some other way?

I am surprised that I am hitting this issue since my model seems of ordinary size, so I would expect that I would not hit this issue unless lots of people were hitting it(?)

issue_title: Tensorboard fails to add summaries without any warning
issue_body:
Hey guys I'm learning to use tensorboard and something wired happens. Tensorboard failed to add summary more than 10 times and showed no warning.Here is my code:
```
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

def rmse(y, y_hat):
    """Compute root mean squared error"""
    return tf.sqrt(tf.reduce_mean(tf.square((y - y_hat))))

def forward(x, e):
    """Forward pass for our fuction"""
    # tensorflow has automatic broadcasting 
    # so we do not need to reshape e manually
    return tf.pow(x, e) 

n = 100 # number of examples
learning_rate = 5e-6

x = tf.placeholder(tf.float32)
y = tf.placeholder(tf.float32)

exp = tf.constant(2.0)
exp_hat = tf.Variable(4.0, name='exp_hat')

y_hat = forward(x, exp_hat)

loss = rmse(y, y_hat)
opt = tf.train.GradientDescentOptimizer(learning_rate)

loss_summary = tf.summary.scalar("loss", loss)
exp_summary = tf.summary.scalar("exp", exp_hat)
all_summaries = tf.summary.merge_all()

train_op = opt.minimize(loss) 

x_train = np.random.rand(n) + 10
y_train = x_train ** 2

loss_history = []
exp_history = []

with tf.Session() as sess:
    
    # Initialize all defined variables
    tf.global_variables_initializer().run()
    
    summary_writer = tf.summary.FileWriter('./tensorboard', sess.graph)
    
    for i in range(0, 500):
        print("Iteration %d" % i)
        summaries, curr_loss, curr_exp, _ = sess.run([all_summaries, loss, exp_hat, train_op], feed_dict={x: x_train, y: y_train})
        
        print("loss = %s" % curr_loss)
        print("exp = %s" % curr_exp)
        
        loss_history.append(curr_loss)
        exp_history.append(curr_exp)
        
        summary_writer.add_summary(summaries, i)
```

The code is from https://medium.com/towards-data-science/pytorch-vs-tensorflow-spotting-the-difference-25c75777377b

The result in console is:
`Iteration 8
loss = 2975.89
exp = 3.36951
Iteration 9
loss = 2721.11
exp = 3.33606`
The program stops here and would not go on without any messages. I did the debug and found it stopped at add_summary(). It's wired because even if I change log frequency to adding 1 summary every 10 iterations, it will still stop at the 90th iteration.
Is it a bug or something?

    if (__name__ == '__main__'):
        # set constants                                                             
        WD = os.getcwd()

estThellierGUI))

breaks two of the common ways that are used to invoke tests.  

    python -m unittest discover # which grabs all the tests in the directory

and

In both cases, `__name__` is not `__main__`, so the necessary constants are not set.  @Caoimhinmg , it looks like these are your changes.  I'm not exactly sure how you're running these tests, but all tests need to work in the two cases above.  There is now some documentation about running tests in pmagpy_tests/README.md

Perhaps the revert directory could be added to the tearDown function instead, and the constants could be set at the top of the file as before?  

I'm having some other issues running these updated tests even if I set constants, but one thing at a time.

issue_title: Curious issue with the FastText python API
issue_body:
I am currently working on a sentiment analysis project, I heard about the FastText classifier and wanted to see how well it performs on my dataset.

So i went through the code proposed on https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fasttext :

Let's say I have my text data and labels stored in 'data', a pandas dataframe
Now i split it into some train/test sets and write them into two .txt files

Each element of my dataset corresponds to one line of the .txt file, with the label as '__label__pos' or '__label__neg' (like in the examples from the fastText documentation)

`from sklearn.cross_validation import train_test_split`
`x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(np.asarray(data['comment']), 
                                                 np.asarray(data['target']), test_size=0.2)`

`with open('train__Data.txt', 'a') as f:`
`     for i,X in enumerate(x_train):`
`        f.write(y_train[i] + ' ' + X)`
`        f.write('\n')`

`with open('test__Data.txt', 'a') as f:`
`     for i,X in enumerate(x_test):`
`        f.write(y_test[i] + ' ' + X)`
`        f.write('\n')`

So now, I should have everything prepared to run the training session.
`import fastext
classifier = fasttext.supervised('train__Data.txt', 'model')`

That piece of code actually does run and takes a little bit of time. It also successfully creates the model.bin file (it does'nt create model.vec, but i've read that was normal since they removed if for the supervised mode)

Now I should be ready to test it and/or get some predictions. Let's first run :
`result = classifier.test('test__Data.txt')
print('P@1:', result.precision)
print('R@1:', result.recall)
print('Number of examples:', result.nexamples)`

Which gives me the following output:

`P@1: nan
R@1: nan
Number of examples: 0`

My .txt files are well encoded as 'utf8'. I tried to experiment fastText with some other goals, like getting some embeddings from a train file and the same thing happened, the code seems to run just fine, takes a little bit of time, but then it can not output any vector representation.

train__Data.txt and test__Data.txt are about 70 and 20 Ko, the model.bin output about 800 Ko. So something does happen, I just can't figure how to test it/print some results!

I've went through the reported issues but couldn't find a similar case.

Am i missing something ?

issue_title: Combination of for_each and group_by
issue_body:
Right now `for_each` cannot be used with `group_by`. More specifically,

* `for_each` repeat input files
* `group_by` repeat input files

The following scenario will fail:

```
[10]
input: for_each=[{'a': range(5)}], group_by='2'
```
because it applies `group_by` to an empty input file list. It would make sense to apply `group_by` to the substeps defined by `for_each`.

issue_title: Release Improvements
issue_body:
The aim of this issue is to track the changes required to simplify the release process and make @cdrage's life easier, or allow others to perform releases.

I think the process should end up being three steps only, via a script or possibly the GitHub UI. 

1. Create PR with updated change log for the new version.
2. Merge PR.
3. Create tag for the new version. 

The rest of the process should be handled automatically, triggered by creating the tag. 

----
 
 - [x] Automatically add the Windows installer to GitHub release (#328)
 - [x] Update the release information using travis from the change log (#328)
 - [x] Include README in the package long_description (#1665)
 - [x] Automatically deploy the `streamlink` package to PyPI (#1701)
 - [x] Automatically add the sdist package GitHub release (#1701)
 - [x] Deploy a wheel to PyPI (#1701)
 - [ ] stable/dev versions of the docs (#1684 WIP)
 - [x] Make the change log consistently markdown, and include it in the docs using `recommonmark` (#1667)
 - [x] Only include installation/open collective in GitHub release info (#1667) 

1. We can include the README.md file in the package metadata as long_description to improve the appearance of the `streamlink` package on PyPI. See https://pypi.org/project/streamlink/0.12.1/ to see how it looks right now.

1. We _could_ deploy the `streamlink` package to PyPI automatically when creating a release tag, this would streamline the release process and remove some of the manual work and possibility for mistakes.

1. We _could_ add the `sdist` package to the GitHub release automatically with the release tag, as we do with the Windows installer. 

1. We _could_ also deploy a wheel to PyPI either automatically or manually, depending on what we do with the others. This might speed up installations in some cases, and is the preferred binary distribution method - but each platform variant might need its own wheel.

1. Separate the docs in to 2 streams (dev and stable), where stable is the default page. A link to the dev version will also need to be added a long with a note stating that is it the development/nightly version. Possibly we could deploy the dev docs at the same time as the nightlies are built, and deploy the stable version on release tag. 

1. The change log is currently a mix of reStructuredText and Markdown, it should be Markdown only. It should be able to be included in the docs by using `recommonmark` in Sphinx to render the Markdown. 

1. The installation instructions and open collective info don't need to be included for every single version in the release log, they are useful in the GitHub releases though. The script that updates the GitHub releases can be modified to include this template, then it doesn't need to be included in the change log. 

As always, it would be nice to have some feedback from the rest of you! I'm sure there are some other things we can improve ­¤śä

issue_title: 'pip install' on our MacOS python 3.5 CI is flaky
issue_body:
Example:

https://ci.cryptography.io/blue/organizations/jenkins/python-trio%2Ftrio/detail/PR-503/12/pipeline/

```
Collecting pyOpenSSL (from -r test-requirements.txt (line 4))

  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pyOpenSSL (from -r test-requirements.txt (line 4)) (from versions: )
```

Example:

https://ci.cryptography.io/blue/organizations/jenkins/python-trio%2Ftrio/detail/PR-503/8/pipeline/

```
Collecting pytest>=3.3 (from -r test-requirements.txt (line 1))

  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pytest>=3.3 (from -r test-requirements.txt (line 1)) (from versions: )
```

This is the same kind of message that you get when using an old pip on an old MacOS, and it can't handle PyPI's new TLS requirements. And it's suspicious that it seems to happen on the Python 3.5 builder only (so far). However... in both of those runs you can see that the first thing we do is use `get-pip.py` to upgrade to pip 10.0.1. The log verifies it:

```
++ pip --version
pip 10.0.1 from /Users/jenkins/workspace/-503-TALZSOV5BV3A6O3/trio/.venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pip (python 3.5)
```

Which makes sense, because if we didn't have working TLS, then we would be 100% unsuccessful at downloading things. Here we're only... intermittently, apparently at random, unsuccessful at downloading things.

I guess the first thing to do is to add some `-vvv` switches to the pip calls and wait for it to happen again...

I won't rule out operator error, but I can't call `list_cluster_role_binding()` without getting rejected:
`ValueError: Invalid value for subjects, must not be None`

The offending method appears to be buried in kubernetes/client/models/xxx_cluster_role_binding.py : 
`if subjects is None:
    raise ValueError("Invalid value for `subjects`, must not be `None`")`

I have no clue how I would pass subjects of objects I'm trying to list, it's certainly not in the docs, at least from reviewing https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/master/kubernetes/docs/RbacAuthorizationV1beta1Api.md#list_cluster_role_binding

Overriding that function does permit listing of cluster role binding objects.  I've attached a gist of a clean interactive session at https://gist.github.com/StephanX/40db23418a3c43e58f00de815c992c42 if it helps.

issue_title: Pagination generator with non-default per_page
issue_body:
Currently if you request `xxx.list(as_list=False, per_page=100) you end up getting a list instead of the generator.

I think the logic in in [http_list](https://github.com/python-gitlab/python-gitlab/blob/7efbc30b9d8cf8ea856b68ab85b9cd2340121358/gitlab/__init__.py#L715) needs some rework.
Perhaps just removing the `'page' in kwargs` condition [from here](https://github.com/python-gitlab/python-gitlab/blob/7efbc30b9d8cf8ea856b68ab85b9cd2340121358/gitlab/__init__.py#L746)

I should be able to specify something like...

```python
my_generator = gl.projects.list(per_page=100, as_list=False)
```

... and still get a generator.  Then I can hook it up to a progress bar like:

```python
my_generator = gl.projects.list(per_page=100, as_list=False, membership=True)
with click.progressbar(my_generator, length=my_generator.total, label='Projects') as bar:
    for project in bar:
        pass
```

The above code generates the following error:

```
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'total'
```

Today if I want to use the generator function I have to leave the `per_page` off and get them only 20 at a time; this makes it much slower.

issue_title: Add support to look up merge requests associated with a commit
issue_body:
## Description of the problem, including code/CLI snippet
Starting in GitLab 10.7, the API now supports retrieving merge requests associated with a commit. 


## Expected Behavior
Ideally, we'd be able to use `python-gitlab` to look up merge requests associated with each commit.

## Actual Behavior
As far as I can tell, this new feature hasn't yet been implemented in Python-Gitlab.


## Specifications

  - python-gitlab version: 1.4.0
  - API version you are using (v3/v4): v4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com): 10.8.2-ee

issue_title: rewrite CLI `predict` function to use model.pipeline
Using pypi installer on fresh systems causes setup to be unable to locate version numbers
issue_body:
The pip install studioml command will not fail during installation but will fail to run python code that relies upon the egg data for the module.  Examples of the error appears below.

The cause is a missing plugin module for the git archive version handling from the requirements.txt file.

traceback (most recent call last):
  File "train_cifar10.py", line 3, in <module>
    from studio import fs_tracker
  File "/tmp/gorun_fzAUcm-kR149823782/experiments/91fe46e69d378fac.0/0WBb2mazg/lib/python2.7/site-packages/studio/__init__.py", line 6, in <module>
    __version__ = get_version(root='..', relative_to=__file__)
  File "/tmp/gorun_fzAUcm-kR149823782/experiments/91fe46e69d378fac.0/0WBb2mazg/lib/python2.7/site-packages/setuptools_scm/__init__.py", line 119, in get_version
    parsed_version = _do_parse(root, parse)
  File "/tmp/gorun_fzAUcm-kR149823782/experiments/91fe46e69d378fac.0/0WBb2mazg/lib/python2.7/site-packages/setuptools_scm/__init__.py", line 97, in _do_parse
    "use git+https://github.com/user/proj.git#egg=proj" % root)
LookupError: setuptools-scm was unable to detect version for '/tmp/gorun_fzAUcm-kR149823782/experiments/91fe46e69d378fac.0/0WBb2mazg/lib/python2.7/site-packages'.

Make sure you're either building from a fully intact git repository or PyPI tarballs. Most other sources (such as GitHub's tarballs, a git checkout without the .git folder) don't contain the necessary metadata and will not work.

For example, if you're using pip, instead of https://github.com/user/proj/archive/master.zip use git+https://github.com/user/proj.git#egg=proj

issue_title: More Customization
issue_body:
Add the option to add custom command line options to linters. For example
```
[command_line:multiline_import]
flake8.ignore += E401
```
If one would now use ``snekchek --multiline_import`` he would run his normal config BUT ignore the flake8 error E401. I think the best way to achieve this would be to use .snekrc as a template for the real config file which could for example be named .snekcache.

issue_title: Incosistent pad attribute on Pad and Conv
issue_body:
Let's make sure it's consistent with the rest of ops and current implementations.

It seems that Pad is right now [x_beg, x_end, y_beg, y_end]: https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/onnx/backend/test/case/node/pad.py
While Conv is [x_beg, y_beg, x_end, y_end]

I'd argue it's confusing and we should change it. I'm a bit biased toward Caffe2 which does [x_beg, y_beg, x_end, y_end] - shall we go with that? The good thing about it is that if you have symmetrical padding it's just list*2

@ebarsoum @yuanbyu

issue_title: Wrong error string for mprof plot run
issue_body:
Hi,

I got the following:

```
$ mprof plot mprofile_20171114100151.dat
matplotlib is needed for plotting.
```

But in fact matplotlib is satisfied:

```
$ sudo pip3.5 install -U matplotlib
Requirement already up-to-date: matplotlib in /usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages
Requirement already up-to-date: pytz in /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
Requirement already up-to-date: cycler>=0.10 in /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
Requirement already up-to-date: numpy>=1.7.1 in /usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
Requirement already up-to-date: python-dateutil>=2.0 in /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
Requirement already up-to-date: six>=1.10 in /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
Requirement already up-to-date: pyparsing!=2.0.4,!=2.1.2,!=2.1.6,>=2.0.1 in /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages (from matplotlib)
```

```
>>> import matplotlib
>>> 
```

From https://github.com/pythonprofilers/memory_profiler/blob/master/mprof#L342

```
def plot_file(filename, index=0, timestamps=True, children=True, options=None):
    try:
        import pylab as pl
    except ImportError as e:
        print("matplotlib is needed for plotting.")
        print(e)
sys.exit(1)
```

So it is printing the string but in fact the mprof failed due to:

```
>>> import pylab
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/pylab.py", line 1, in <module>
    from matplotlib.pylab import *
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/matplotlib/pylab.py", line 257, in <module>
    from matplotlib import cbook, mlab, pyplot as plt
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/matplotlib/pyplot.py", line 113, in <module>
    _backend_mod, new_figure_manager, draw_if_interactive, _show = pylab_setup()
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/matplotlib/backends/__init__.py", line 60, in pylab_setup
    [backend_name], 0)
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/matplotlib/backends/backend_tkagg.py", line 6, in <module>
    from six.moves import tkinter as Tk
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/six.py", line 92, in __get__
    result = self._resolve()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/six.py", line 115, in _resolve
    return _import_module(self.mod)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/six.py", line 82, in _import_module
    __import__(name)
ImportError: No module named 'tkinter'
```

Based on this, it will be great to give much more informative error message here.

issue_title: Wrong parse bug
issue_body:
I found a [bug](http://nodeca.github.com/js-yaml/#yaml=CmJ1Z3M6CiAgeDogMSAjIG9rCiAgeTogMiAjIGJ1Z2d5LCBleHBlY3Qga2V5ICd5JwogIG46IDMgIyBidWdneSwgZXhwZWN0IGtleSAnbicKICB5ZXNWYWx1ZTogeSAjIGJ1Z2d5LCBleHBlY3Qgc3RyaW5nICd5JwogIG5vVmFsdWU6IG4gIyBidWdneSwgZXhwZWN0IHN0cmluZyAnbicKIAogIA==) Keys or values `y` evaluate as true (yes), the same bug with `n`: 

bugs:
  x: 1 # ok
  y: 2 # buggy, expect key 'y'
  n: 3 # buggy, expect key 'n'
  yesValue: y # buggy, expect string 'y'
  noValue: n # buggy, expect string 'n'
-> 
{ bugs: 
   { x: 1,
     true: 2,
     false: 3,
     yesValue: true,
     noValue: false } }
